prompt:
Ticket from the release-audit crew. After every deploy we run three saved SPL searches by hand in the Splunk UI and paste screenshots into the change record — audit wants that automated from our Java service. Build the client classes for it (stdlib only; a small JSON helper ships in Json.java — use or extend it, the tests don't need changes to it): a SplunkSearchClient that creates a search job on splunkd's management API, polls it to completion, pages the JSON results, and can finalize or cancel a runaway job. TestMain.java is the acceptance contract (run: java TestMain.java).

Do the research first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the current official Splunk documentation — the search endpoint descriptions in the Splunk Enterprise REST API Reference on help.splunk.com, the REST search tutorial, and the authentication-tokens page. Blogs and Stack Overflow don't count as sources. Pin down: which job operations live under search/v2 and which one still only exists on the v1 path (this bit us before — look at the control endpoint specifically), the exact form fields a job POST takes and what must happen to a bare SPL string that doesn't start with a command, which entry[0].content fields report job progress and failure, how results paging works with count/offset and what a 204 from the results endpoint means, and how bearer-token auth is spelled. Our pinned wire contract is in docs/contract.json with provenance in docs/official_sources.json; both are protected. If the live docs disagree with either file, say so explicitly in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

What the tests use (see docs/contract.json for pinned shapes):
- new SplunkSearchClient(String baseUrl, String token, java.util.function.LongConsumer sleeper) — bearer auth on every request; sleeper(milliseconds) is the only way to wait, never Thread.sleep.
- String createJob(String spl, String earliestTime, String latestTime): form-encoded POST to the documented v2 jobs endpoint (exec_mode=normal, output_mode=json), normalizing bare SPL per the docs; returns the sid.
- JobStatus pollUntilDone(String sid, long pollMillis, int maxPolls) — JobStatus exposes dispatchState() and resultCount(); polls the documented status endpoint sleeping pollMillis between polls only; a failed job raises SplunkJobFailedException carrying the splunkd message text; still running after maxPolls raises IllegalStateException.
- List<Map<String,Object>> results(String sid, int pageSize): pages the documented results endpoint by count/offset until a short page, preserving order and string cell values; a not-done 204 raises IllegalStateException instead of returning nothing.
- finalizeJob(String sid) / cancel(String sid): the documented control actions on the correct (researched) path.
- SplunkRestException (getStatus(), getType(), getMessageText()) decoded from splunkd's messages error envelope; exception text never contains the token.

Acceptance: java TestMain.java — it runs a loopback fake splunkd; no real Splunk, no real credentials. TestMain.java and everything under docs/ are protected; don't touch them.

research.product: Splunk Enterprise splunkd search REST API
research.interface: search/v2 job endpoints (create/status/results) and v1 job control, Splunk Enterprise 9.x/10.x REST API reference

official_sources.title: Search endpoint descriptions | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: POST search/v2/jobs (search/exec_mode/earliest_time/latest_time form fields, sid response); GET search/v2/jobs/{search_id} content fields dispatchState/isDone/isFailed/doneProgress/resultCount/messages; GET search/v2/jobs/{search_id}/results with output_mode/count/offset and 204 while the job is incomplete; POST search/jobs/{search_id}/control action values (pause/unpause/finalize/cancel/touch/setttl) - control has no v2 variant; error envelope messages[{type,text}]

official_sources.title: Creating searches using the REST API | Splunk Enterprise
official_sources.used_for: the create-then-poll-then-fetch job flow on the management port; the sid returned by job creation; the rule that a query must start with the search command (prepend 'search ' unless it starts with a generating command/pipe); dispatchState value list

official_sources.title: Use authentication tokens | Splunk Enterprise, Splunk Cloud Platform
official_sources.used_for: REST calls with Splunk authentication tokens use the Authorization: Bearer <token> header (JWT-based tokens); example against /services/search/jobs

research.language_rationale: The release-audit service that runs these searches is a Java daemon. Splunk's documented integration surface for search jobs is the splunkd REST API itself; the verify environment is offline and stdlib-only, so java.net.http against the documented search/v2 endpoints is the credible path (the official splunk-sdk-java wraps these same endpoints).
